race_name: Tour of Thekkady — Kerala Gran Fondo

top_categories.workout_context: At mile 60 of 90, glycogen is gone. This workout teaches your body to produce watts on fumes.
109ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
Tour of Thekkady — Kerala Gran Fondo's mixed terrain forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 90 miles.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Tour of Thekkady — Kerala Gran Fondo's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before March.
At Tour of Thekkady — Kerala Gran Fondo, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Tour of Thekkady — Kerala Gran Fondo. Train it until it feels boring.
9,800ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 90 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
9,800ft of climbing on grades that change every minute. You need to switch from seated to standing without losing rhythm.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At Tour of Thekkady — Kerala Gran Fondo, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.
The aerobic base that lets you ride mixed terrain at tempo for 90 miles without cracking. No shortcuts.
Low-cadence force work for 9,800ft of climbing. The slow, grinding ascents where you can't spin—you push.

race_overlay.heat: March in Kottayam to Thekkady, Kerala, India can bring heat. Complete 3–4 heat exposure sessions in the final 2 weeks before Tour of Thekkady — Kerala Gran Fondo. Increase sodium intake 48 hours before race day.
race_overlay.nutrition: Target 60–80g carbs/hour for Tour of Thekkady — Kerala Gran Fondo’s 90 miles. Start fueling within the first 30 minutes — early fueling prevents late-race collapse. Bonking at mile 60 is a nutrition failure, not a fitness failure.
race_overlay.altitude: Moderate altitude at Tour of Thekkady — Kerala Gran Fondo. Arrive 2–3 days early. Reduce intensity expectations by 5–10%. Hydrate aggressively.
race_overlay.terrain: Mixed terrain at Tour of Thekkady — Kerala Gran Fondo requires surface adaptability. Include weekly rides on mixed terrain to build confidence.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Durability, TT Threshold, and Gravel Specific to prepare you for 90 miles of mixed terrain in Kottayam to Thekkady, Kerala, India.
